data.content:
### Apply and reset local Supabase database migrations (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/local-development/overview.mdx

The `supabase migration up` command applies any pending migration files to your local database, updating its schema. Alternatively, `supabase db reset` completely purges and recreates the local database, reapplying all migrations and seed data.

```bash
# To apply the new migration to your local database:
supabase migration up

# To reset your local database completely:
supabase db reset
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Supabase database migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2023-08-08-supabase-local-dev.mdx

The `supabase migration` command provides a robust way to manage database schema changes using version-controlled SQL files. It offers subcommands for listing migrations, creating new scripts, repairing the migration history, squashing multiple migrations into one, and applying pending migrations to the local database.

```markdown
supabase migration
Manage database migration scripts

Usage:
  supabase migration [command]

Available Commands:
  list        List local and remote migrations
  new         Create an empty migration script
  repair      Repair the migration history table
  squash      Squash migrations to a single file
  up          Apply pending migrations to local database
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy database migrations to Supabase

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Push your local database migrations to the remote Supabase database. This synchronizes your schema changes to the deployed project.

```bash
supabase db push
```

--------------------------------

### Create Supabase Migration from Existing File (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/managing-environments.mdx

Generates a new migration file by piping the content of an existing SQL script file (e.g., `create_employees_table.sql`) into the `supabase migration new` command. This allows reusing pre-written SQL for migrations.

```bash
supabase migration new new_employee < create_employees_table.sql
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy database migrations and seed data to Supabase

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Push both your database migrations and any configured seed data to the remote Supabase database. This is useful for initializing the database with essential data after schema changes.

```bash
supabase db push --include-seed
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Migration for Table Modification

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Create a new migration file to introduce changes to an existing table, such as adding a new column to the 'employees' table. This command prepares a new migration for subsequent SQL definitions.

```bash
supabase migration new add_department_column
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/projects/{ref}/database/migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/integrations/supabase-for-platforms.mdx

Execute database migrations on a Supabase project. This endpoint automatically creates a migration inside the supabase_migrations schema and runs it. If the migration fails, changes are automatically rolled back. Access to this endpoint is restricted to select customers.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/projects/{ref}/database/migrations

### Description
Execute database migrations on a Supabase project. The endpoint automatically creates a migration in the supabase_migrations schema and executes it. Failed migrations are automatically rolled back.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/projects/{ref}/database/migrations

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **ref** (string) - Required - The project reference ID

#### Request Body
- **query** (string) - Required - SQL query or queries to execute
- **name** (string) - Required - Name of the migration

### Request Example
```json
{
  "query": "create table public.todos (id serial primary key, task text not null); alter table public.todos enable row level security;",
  "name": "Create a todos table"
}
```

### cURL Example
```sh
curl https://api.supabase.com/v1/projects/{ref}/database/migrations \
  --request POST \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_SECRET_TOKEN' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{
    "query": "create table public.todos (id serial primary key, task text not null); alter table public.todos enable row level security;",
    "name": "Create a todos table"
  }'
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Migration execution status
- **migration_id** (string) - Unique identifier for the migration

### Notes
- Only select customers have access to this endpoint
- Submit access request form to get access to migrations endpoint
```

--------------------------------

### Fix Supabase Migration Order Problems

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/branching/troubleshooting.mdx

To resolve issues with migration order, such as timestamp conflicts or rebase problems, rename migration files to correct their sequential order based on timestamps. After adjusting file names, reset the local Supabase database to reapply all migrations correctly.

```bash
mv 20240101000000_old.sql 20240102000000_old.sql
```

```bash
supabase db reset
```

--------------------------------

### Fix Auth Migration Error - UUID Type Mismatch in Supabase

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/troubleshooting/an-invalid-response-was-received-from-the-upstream-server-error-when-querying-auth-RI4Vl-.mdx

This SQL command resolves the 'operator does not exist: uuid = text' error by manually inserting a migration record into the auth.schema_migrations table. This prevents Supabase Auth from re-running the problematic migration 20221208132122_backfill_email_last_sign_in_at.up.sql. Execute this via the Supabase SQL editor when encountering auth service failures due to migration issues.

```sql
insert into auth.schema_migrations values ('20221208132122');
```

--------------------------------

### Add New Column to Table in SQL Migration

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Insert SQL into the latest migration file to add a 'department' column to the 'employees' table. This column is defined as text with a default value of 'Hooli'.

```sql
alter table if exists public.employees
add department text default 'Hooli';
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Database Migrations to Production Environment with GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/managing-environments.mdx

This workflow deploys database migrations to a Supabase production project when code is pushed to the main branch. It uses production-specific environment variables (PRODUCTION_DB_PASSWORD and PRODUCTION_PROJECT_ID) stored as GitHub secrets to link and push migration changes to the production database.

```yaml
name: Deploy Migrations to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    env:
      SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
      SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_DB_PASSWORD }}
      SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_PROJECT_ID }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: supabase/setup-cli@v1
        with:
          version: latest

      - run: supabase link --project-ref $SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID
      - run: supabase db push
```

--------------------------------

### Define Employee Table in SQL Migration

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Add SQL statements to the newly created migration file to define the 'employees' table. This table includes columns for ID, name, email, and creation timestamp.

```sql
create table if not exists employees (
  id bigint primary key generated always as identity,
  name text not null,
  email text,
  created_at timestamptz default now()
);
```

--------------------------------

### Rebase Local Supabase Migrations with Git

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/managing-environments.mdx

This sequence of commands fetches the latest changes from Git, creates a new migration file, renames an existing local migration file to match a new timestamp, and then resets the local Supabase database. This process helps incorporate new upstream migrations while preserving local schema changes and resolving potential conflicts.

```bash
git pull
supabase migration new dev_A
mv <time>_dev_A.sql <t+2>_dev_A.sql
supabase db reset
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Supabase Migration File from Schema Diff (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

This command generates a new migration file by comparing the current database schema with its previous state. The `-f` flag names the output file, allowing developers to capture schema changes made via the Supabase Dashboard.

```bash
supabase db diff -f create_cities_table
```

--------------------------------

### Run Laravel database migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/getting-started/quickstarts/laravel.mdx

Execute Laravel's database migrations to create the necessary tables for authentication and user management in your connected database. This command applies the schema changes defined in your migration files. Note that Laravel's authentication operates independently of Supabase Auth.

```bash
php artisan migrate
```

--------------------------------

### Create Supabase Migration File

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Generate a new migration file using the Supabase CLI to store the SQL definition for a new table, such as an 'employees' table. This command creates an empty migration file in your project's migrations directory.

```bash
supabase migration new create_employees_table
```

--------------------------------

### Synchronize PostgreSQL Sequences During Migration

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/platform/migrating-to-supabase/postgres.mdx

This set of commands handles the synchronization of sequences (like auto-incrementing IDs) after the initial data transfer. It involves setting the source database to read-only, exporting only the sequence data from the source using `pg_dump`, and then importing it into the Supabase database using `psql` to ensure consistency.

```bash
# Set source to read-only
psql -h <source_host> -c "ALTER DATABASE <source_database> SET default_transaction_read_only = true;"
```

```bash
# Export sequences from source
pg_dump \
  -h <source_host> \
  -U <source_user> \
  -p <source_port> \
  -d <source_database> \
  --data-only \
  --table='*_seq' \
  --table='*_id_seq' > sequences.sql
```

```bash
# Import sequences to Supabase
psql "$SUPABASE_DB_URL" -f sequences.sql
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Supabase Migration to Create Table

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/database-migrations.mdx

Execute the pending migration files using the Supabase CLI to apply the SQL changes to your database. This command will create the 'employees' table as defined in the migration file.

```bash
supabase migration up
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Database Migrations to Staging Environment with GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/deployment/managing-environments.mdx

This workflow automatically deploys database migrations to a Supabase staging project when code is pushed to the develop branch. It requires SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN, STAGING_DB_PASSWORD, and STAGING_PROJECT_ID as encrypted secrets and uses the Supabase CLI to link and push changes.

```yaml
name: Deploy Migrations to Staging

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - develop
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    env:
      SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
      SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DB_PASSWORD }}
      SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.STAGING_PROJECT_ID }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: supabase/setup-cli@v1
        with:
          version: latest

      - run: supabase link --project-ref $SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID
      - run: supabase db push
```

--------------------------------

### Demonstrate NO ACTION INITIALLY DEFERRED precedence with CASCADE in PostgreSQL

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/database/postgres/cascade-deletes.mdx

This example shows how `ON DELETE INITIALLY DEFERRED` behaves when another `ON DELETE CASCADE` constraint is present on the same table. The `CASCADE` constraint takes precedence, leading to successful deletion of parent and child records. The subsequent `SELECT` queries confirm the deletion of all related rows.

```shell
delete from grandparent;

select * from parent;

select * from child;
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Firebase to Supabase migration repository using Git

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/platform/migrating-to-supabase/firestore-data.mdx

Clones the firebase-to-supabase GitHub repository containing migration tools. This repository provides utilities for converting Firestore collections to Postgres tables. Required as the first step before configuring the migration.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/supabase-community/firebase-to-supabase.git
```
